full_text: Odean Smith to Munsey, , edgeddd and it escapes between keeper and short third man. Short of a length delivery angling across, Munsey moves across and slogs, gets lucky with the edge and the Scotland fans are happy with 160 on the board

full_text: Odean Smith to Munsey, , good length delivery, Munsey slogs, manages to middle it over mid-wicket and it's in the gap. Much needed hit for Munsey. First boundary for him since 35 balls

full_text: Alzarri Joseph to Chris Greaves, 2 runs, full delivery, Greaves drives square of the wicket on the off-side, poor throw to the keeper and they concede an overthrow too

short_text: Alzarri Joseph to Chris Greaves, leg byes, 1 run, oh brilliant diving save from Pooran. Short delivery, Greaves bends to
full_text: Alzarri Joseph to Chris Greaves, leg byes, 1 run, oh brilliant diving save from Pooran. Short delivery, Greaves bends to scoop this over fine leg, Alzarri Joseph bowls the bouncer, the ball brushed the side of the helmet and goes wide of the keeper but Pooran dives. He has the awareness to try for the runout as Munsey is slow, would have been out had he hit direct. Physio comes out for concussion test

short_text: Alzarri Joseph to Munsey, leg byes, 1 run, Munsey moves across and tries to scoop, misses the ball completely. Gets hit
full_text: Alzarri Joseph to Munsey, leg byes, 1 run, Munsey moves across and tries to scoop, misses the ball completely. Gets hit on the pad, easily pitching outside leg, which is why umpire Aleem Dar walked away from the appeal

full_text: Alzarri Joseph to Munsey, no run, short delivery, Munsey looks to slash and misses

full_text: Alzarri Joseph to Munsey, no run, very lucky there Munsey. Thankfully it wasn't those cunning very marginal umpire's call thing on ball-tracker. Alzarri Joseph missed the yorker and about a thigh high full toss, Munsey swung and missed. Sure would have clipped some part of leg stump but impact was outside leg

full_text: Obed McCoy to Chris Greaves, , that would have been straight down the throat had Akeal Hosein been back on the rope. He was a long way in standing at fine leg and to make it even more silly, it lands inside the rope. Short delivery, Greaves pulls and gets a top edge, gets lucky

full_text: Obed McCoy to Chris Greaves, , lovely! Short delivery, Greaves moves across and sends this away fine over fine leg despite being on one knee. Brave hit that

full_text: Alzarri Joseph to Leask, Bowled!! Knocked him over. 143kph yorker, Leask backs away and looks to square drive, misses and the ball hits the base of middle stump. Only middle stump. That's how accurate that ball was.

full_text: Odean Smith to MacLeod, Caught by Brandon King!! Oh he has smashed this straight to cover. Pace off from Odean Smith, simple length delivery, MacLeod read that, backed away and slapped it hard, but didn't get the placement right. Walks off livid with himself. End of a good cameo.

full_text: Alzarri Joseph to Berrington, Caught by Mayers!! Taken at short mid-wicket. Pacy short delivery, again Berrington is rushed for pace and bounce. The ball hits the splice of the bat as he looks to pull, simple catch for Mayers. Key wicket for West Indies.

full_text: Alzarri Joseph to Munsey, 1 run, pulled away nicely, but man at deep backward square leg does really well running to the left to stop that boundary. Just one run

full_text: Alzarri Joseph to Berrington, 1 run, oh rushed him there with a short delivery, Munsey calls for a risky run and literally sold Berrington down the river. full_text: Holder to Matthew Cross, Caught by Shamarh Brooks!! Holder has two. Hits the deck hard with a back of a length delivery outside off, Cross looks to swat it over the infield but could only get it as far as mid-on. Brooks times his jump to his left and takes a well-judged catch. Not a great deal of pace from Holder and it clearly seem to be frustrating the batters.

full_text: Holder to Michael Jones, Bowled!! Here comes the first wicket. I0$ Test-match stuff. Wobble seam that nips back from a good length and beats Michael Jones all ends up on the inside edge or has he? Might have been totally baffled by the late inward seam movement and ended up getting a healthy inside edge that deflects back onto the stumps.

full_text: Holder to Munsey, leg byes, 1 run, misses the flick once again, but this one had pitched outside leg and deflects to mid-wicket off the toes

full_text: Obed McCoy to Munsey, leg byes, 1 run, full inswinger just outside off, Munsey shuffled forward and across before getting beaten on the inside edge. full_text: Obed McCoy to Michael Jones, 1 run, slower full delivery at I0$ outside off, Jones pings the drive to the left off and takes off for a quick single. A direct hit at the bowling end and the batters ponder an extra run on the overthrow but decide against it...

full_text: Obed McCoy to Munsey, leg byes, 1 run, gee, that looked very tight on replays. If it wasn't for that usual extra bounce on these surfaces, George Munsey would have been a goner. He shuffled a long way across and failed to get bat on the attempted hoick, it swung in and crashed him on the inside of the front pad. Skipper Pooran reviews after a brief consultation with the bowler, but the ..

full_text: Mayers to Munsey, leg byes, 1 run, tries to hoick a short of length delivery down the leg-side and misses, it clips the thigh pad and rolls to square leg

full_text: Mayers to Munsey, no run, makes room and slices the drive to the left of backward point, Smith nearly made a mess of that fielding effort but did well to recover in time

full_text: Mayers to Munsey, , ah George Munsey is a specialist at playing these whippy flicks behind square on the leg-side. This one drifts into the pads and makes Munsey's task much easier, just a whippy aerial flick and it goes racing to the backward square leg fence

full_text: Mayers to Munsey, , Munsey uses his feet, picks the full away swinger off the pads and plays the pick up flick behind square on the leg-side. Superb timing!

full_text: Mayers to Michael Jones, no run, Slightly tough, but should have been taken. Michael Jones made room on the off-side and the inward movement on this inswinger meant he couldn't really get leg-side of the ball, checks the drive off the toe end and Mayers couldn't hold on with his one-handed attempt to his right

full_text: Mayers to Michael Jones, , sweet timing once again! Made heaps of runs at Durham this year and no wonder he is showing his form. Another fullie outside off and it's been creamed through extra cover for another boundary

short_text: Mayers to Michael Jones, , a low full toss outside off and it's been sweetly timed past mid-off's right. A check drive f
full_text: Mayers to Michael Jones, , a low full toss outside off and it's been sweetly timed past mid-off's right. A check drive from Michael Jones and that was all that was needed from the Scotland opener

full_text: Mayers to Munsey, 1 run, gentle I0$ length delivery outside off, punched away through covers and Munsey is off the mark with a single

short_text: Safyaan Sharif to Holder, Caught by Chris Greaves!! In the airrrr, taken! Yesterday Namibia, today Scotland. What a star
full_text: Safyaan Sharif to Holder, Caught by Chris Greaves!! In the airrrr, taken! Yesterday Namibia, today Scotland. What a start we have had to the World Cup. With all due respect to West Indies, for the way they played today, this can't be called an upset. Safyaan Sharif took pace off and bowled full, Holder tried to loft this over long-off, sliced it. Man at long-off ran in and dived low to take a very good catch.

full_text: Safyaan Sharif to Obed McCoy, 1 run, slower delivery, McCoy looks to slog, gets an outside edge that goes on the bounce to third man

full_text: Safyaan Sharif to Holder, 1 run, full toss, Holder drags it to deep mid-wicket, just a single. Interesting he took that

full_text: Davey to Holder, 2 runs, really full delivery, Holder digs it out past cover, two more

full_text: Davey to Holder, , this time a wide full toss, Holder leans forward and just places the ball, look at that timing. Backward point dived in vain and it is racing off

full_text: Davey to Holder, , full toss, need more of these, the West Indies. Holder smashes it over mid-wicket

full_text: Mark Watt to Obed McCoy, no run, darted from round the wicket, McCoy defends

short_text: Mark Watt to Holder, 1 run, oh ho ho! What a ball to finish a fantastic spell, ah still a ball left. So Mark Watt was al
full_text: Mark Watt to Holder, 1 run, oh ho ho! What a ball to finish a fantastic spell, ah still a ball left. So Mark Watt was all about firing them flat and full, so Holder was ready for something like that. Smartly he floats one high and deceives a set Holder, luckily for the batter the chip to cover is safe because there is no one in the circle

full_text: Mark Watt to Holder, , tries the wide yorker but fires way outside off stump

full_text: Mark Watt to Holder, no run, yorker, Holder digs it out

full_text: Mark Watt to Obed McCoy, 1 run, full delivery, McCoy drives to deep cover

short_text: Mark Watt to Odean Smith, Caught by Chris Greaves!! Catch it is the cry, taken really well overhead with some backward s
full_text: Mark Watt to Odean Smith, Caught by Chris Greaves!! Catch it is the cry, taken really well overhead with some backward steps. Again Mark Watt delivers from behind the stumps, full ball, so Smith thought he could tee off. Hit it pretty well but hit it flat. Long-off was in and this could flown over but Greaves was very good. Third wicket for Watt. He says I have this in check.

short_text: Wheal to Holder, , brilliant batting! Wheal went full, but Holder didn't try to muscle the ball, just placed it nicely p
full_text: Wheal to Holder, , brilliant batting! Wheal went full, but Holder didn't try to muscle the ball, just placed it nicely past backward point and deep cover had no chance. This is still doable for West Indies if someone can hang around with Holder

full_text: Wheal to Holder, no run, short of length delivery down leg, Holder swings and misses

full_text: Wheal to Holder, , one of the sweetest timers of the cricket ball in world cricket. Doesn't give you the impression, but his hits stay hit. Slot delivery, front leg out of the way and smashed over mid-wicket

short_text: Mark Watt to Alzarri Joseph, Caught by Matthew Cross!! Edgeddd and gone! Good catch and Alzarri Joseph can't believe his
full_text: Mark Watt to Alzarri Joseph, Caught by Matthew Cross!! Edgeddd and gone! Good catch and Alzarri Joseph can't believe his eyes. Mark Watt delivered that from way behind the crease like he often does. Was a simple flatter delivery outside off stump, Alzarri was late on the cut shot, got a thick deflection which is was very well held. 79/8. Still Smith to follow.

short_text: Leask to Holder, no run, plays inside the line and misses
full_text: Leask to Holder, no run, plays inside the line and misses

full_text: Leask to Holder, no run, flatter delivery, Holder defends

short_text: Leask to Holder, Akeal Hosein Run Out!! Where were you running, Akeal Hosein? Holder just knocked this beside the pitch,
full_text: Leask to Holder, Akeal Hosein Run Out!! Where were you running, Akeal Hosein? Holder just knocked this beside the pitch, Hosein took off when it was absolutely not needed and Holder was looking to bat through to take his team home. Just needed someone to support him, but his partner thought he could steal a run and he was too far down before he hit the brakes. So far down that Leask, despite missing the stumps first attempt, had enough time to flick his hands back to ignite the poles. Smart of the keeper to quickly attack and fire it to the bowler. Scotland sense something here!

short_text: Wheal to Shamarh Brooks, Caught by Leask!! In the airrr, taken. What a good diving catch. This could have gone to ground
full_text: Wheal to Shamarh Brooks, Caught by Leask!! In the airrr, taken. What a good diving catch. This could have gone to ground had he not been very alert and timed the dive to perfection. Simple short delivery, wasn't all that short, could have been hit but Brooks only wanted to pull this aerial. Mistimed the shot, but Leask judged that to perfection. Six down! Well, well!

short_text: Leask to Powell, Caught by MacLeod!! That has gone a long way high in the air, but isn't big enough. Taken. What scenes
full_text: Leask to Powell, Caught by MacLeod!! That has gone a long way high in the air, but isn't big enough. Taken. What scenes for Scotland. Oh West Indies, what are on earth are you doing? Teasing stuff this from Leask in an age where teams tend to hold back right-arm offies to right-hander power-hitters. He bowled this really full, actually in the slot and asked Powell to tee off. Powell did, sure the shot was on, but unfortunately for the batter he toe-ended this. For a moment it looked he hit that out of the ground. But it's 69/5. The key to this wicket was Powell slanted that across and Powell had slightly fetch the ball.

full_text: Leask to Powell, 2 runs, tosses it full and wide of off, Powell slices the loft over extra cover and gets off the mark with a double. Shouts of 'catch it' but there's no-one in the deep in front of square on the off-side

short_text: Leask to Pooran, Bowled!! This is a serious problem for Nic Pooran. The number of times he's out to spin (off-spin in pa
full_text: Leask to Pooran, Bowled!! This is a serious problem for Nic Pooran. The number of times he's out to spin (off-spin in particular) in the recent past - got to be some mind-boggling stat there. It was slightly short and not short enough to play the slog/pull. Pooran jumped on the back foot looking to find a boundary with a hack and the lack of bounce beats his under edge, hits the top of off and sends Scotland into a cock-a-hoop mood.

full_text: Leask to Pooran, no run, quicker and wider of off, Pooran misses out on the cut and the ball deflects onto the stumps off the keeper's pad. Mild appeal and the umpire goes upstairs...

full_text: Mark Watt to Shamarh Brooks, leg byes, 1 run, fires in the slider down leg, Brooks misses the flick and it runs away to the right of short fine off the pads

short_text: Mark Watt to Brandon King, Bowled!! Cleans him up. Mark Watt says leave it to me, I'll take care of everything. It's the
full_text: Mark Watt to Brandon King, Bowled!! Cleans him up. Mark Watt says leave it to me, I'll take care of everything. It's the gesture we are talking about here. Fired it quicker and hit the top of off. The length was neither full nor shot, pushed Brandon King onto the back foot and gave him no room whatsoever. Got the ball to skid through and beat him on the cut.

short_text: Wheal to Lewis, Caught by Michael Jones at backward square leg!! Lewis didn't look at his best today and he falls trying
full_text: Wheal to Lewis, Caught by Michael Jones at backward square leg!! Lewis didn't look at his best today and he falls trying to play one of his go-to shots - the pull - in T20 cricket. Wheal bowled the short ball into the deck and didn't offer as much pace, Lewis swivels and drags the pull off the thick inside half and picks out the feidler at deep backward square to absolute perfection.

short_text: Davey to Mayers, Caught by Munsey!! Excellent catch by Munsey. Davey shortens the length and cuts the pace while angling
full_text: Davey to Mayers, Caught by Munsey!! Excellent catch by Munsey. Davey shortens the length and cuts the pace while angling it into the left-hander, Mayers tries for a whippy flick but doesn't time it as well as he would have liked. Skews it off the thick inside half and the fielder from mid-wicket runs across to take a fine sliding catch to his left.

short_text: Davey to Mayers, , too easy! A gimme on the pads and he's helped that away for a over backward square. Got it off the to
full_text: Davey to Mayers, , too easy! A gimme on the pads and he's helped that away for a over backward square. Got it off the toe but there's enough pace to go the distance

full_text: Davey to Mayers, , full pitcher outside off, Mayers leans on the front foot and lifts the drive back over the bowler's head. The fielder from long-on runs across and puts in a desperate dive but he ended up making contact with the ropes while at it...
